name: Tower
notes: Standalone structure, not tied to any base — built anywhere by an Engineer. Doesn't consume a base's population slot since it isn't placed at a base. Clears fog of war at a high vision range independent of its attack range. Three variants play genuinely differently rather than a flat cost/power tradeoff: Stone Tower is high damage/low attack speed, simple stat scaling per level. Wood Tower is low damage/high attack speed, Fire-vulnerable (like Wood Walls/Bridges/Docks), and each upgrade level adds an additional independently-targeting turret instead of scaling its own stats — built for clearing swarms/fast harassers rather than single strong threats. Steel Tower trades away Air targeting entirely and drops damage/range below Stone's, but adds flat armor on top of by far the highest hp of the three, and its hit splashes (splashRadius 2, via defensiveStatsOverride) like a Grenade Tower — it's a bunker for soaking and clearing ground pushes, not a well-rounded defense. Like Walls, a destroyed Tower deletes outright rather than ruining — rebuilding it is a fresh build at full cost, never a discounted ruin-rebuild (so rebuildCost does not apply here). Also the first Defensive building to double as a stealth detector (detector: true, detectionRange: 3) — a much shorter radius than its 12-tile visionRange, since normal fog-of-war clearing and spotting a cloaked Ghost Tank/Submarine are different senses. Unlike Radar Array (Signal Ridge's single, fixed, long-range detector with a map-wide vision bonus), a Tower is cheap, placeable anywhere, and built in numbers — its short detectionRange means it only counters stealth near itself, so a Ghost Tank/Submarine stays viable everywhere except waters/approaches a player has specifically walled and towered. That gives Tower a second purpose worth defending in its own right, rather than just being a generic chokepoint piece. Revealing a stealthed unit exposes it to ANY of the owning player's nearby troops/buildings, not just this Tower's own attack — a lone Tower can lose a straight fight to a full squad (e.g. 4 Light Tanks' combined dps kills a 300hp Stone Tower in seconds), so its job is stripping the stealth for a garrison/Wall/other defenses to finish, not necessarily soloing the kill itself. Against a single stealthed unit its own stats hold up fine (higher HP/damage and longer range than e.g. Light Tank).
